name: Bridge Dental
tagline: Prompts for your whole dental practice

safety.phi: Safety rule: Use no patient identifiers. Never include patient names, dates of birth, addresses, phone numbers, chart/account numbers, or insurance IDs — use [bracket] placeholders instead. This output is a first draft; review it before anyone (especially a patient) sees it.
safety.confidential: Safety rule: Keep confidential data out of this prompt. Don't include account or card numbers, bank details, patient names, individual employee pay, EINs, or other sensitive specifics — redact them or use [bracket] placeholders. Treat the output as a first draft and sanity-check the numbers before acting on them.
safety.verify: Verify rule: AI-found figures and estimates are a starting point — confirm against primary sources, and with your CPA and attorney, before any offer, purchase, or filing.

patient.askClause: Before you write, ask me 2–3 quick questions to get the details right.
patient.pasteTemplate:
Here is the {label} to work from. Paste it between the lines below, with any patient names or identifiers removed — use [brackets] instead:
---
[ paste the {label} here ]
---

advisor.askClause: Before you start, ask me 2–3 quick questions to get the details right.
advisor.researchNote: Use a web-connected AI tool for this (ChatGPT with search, Gemini, Grok, or Claude with web) so it can look up current data.
advisor.pasteTemplate:
Attach the {label} in your AI tool, or paste it between the lines below. First remove anything confidential — account or card numbers, patient names, individual employee pay, bank details — or replace it with [brackets]:
---
[ attach or paste the {label} here ]
---

tasks.text: Respond to a negative review (safe)
tasks.detail: Never confirm whether the reviewer is a patient, share no treatment or billing details, and invite a private conversation.
followups: Make it warmer without admitting fault.

tasks.text: Respond to a one-star review that mentions clinical details
tasks.detail: Acknowledge their feelings and stay gracious. Never confirm the person is a patient, share no treatment or billing specifics, avoid anything defensive, and invite a private conversation.
followups: Make it warmer without admitting fault.
Draft the private message to send them directly.
Give me a reusable template for reviews like this.

tasks.text: Calm a complaint that's going viral
tasks.detail: Write one calm, non-defensive public reply under 60 words, then give three internal next-step bullets including private outreach. No arguing in the thread, and no patient details.
followups: Draft the internal do-say / don't-say brief for the team.
Write the private outreach message to the original poster.
What should we watch over the next 48 hours?

roles.label: Fractional CFO (money & costs)
roles.prompt: a fractional CFO for a dental practice
contexts: supply and lab costs keep creeping up
a typical month at the practice

tasks.text: Audit credit-card and bank statements for savings
followups: Rank the savings by annual dollar impact.
Draft cancellation emails for the top three.
What should I watch for on next month's statement?

tasks.text: Review the P&L and flag categories that need attention
followups: Which single category should I attack first?
What would each fix be worth annually?
Draft the email or script to act on your top recommendation.

tasks.text: Compare a dental-supply invoice against other vendors
tasks.detail: Normalize every line to a true unit cost (price divided by units per pack) so the comparison is apples to apples, name the best vendor per item, and total the potential annual savings.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Find recurring subscriptions and charges to cancel
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Cut accounts-receivable over 90 days
followups: Script the first call for the biggest balance.
Which claims should we resubmit versus write off?
Build a weekly A/R routine so this doesn't pile up again.

tasks.text: Analyze which PPO plans to keep or drop and model a fee increase
tasks.input: fee schedule and insurance write-off report
followups: Model dropping the single worst plan — what's the patient-loss break-even?
Draft the patient letter announcing the change.
What's the safest timeline and order of operations?

tasks.text: Audit lab bills for overcharges
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Check production vs. collections for leakage
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Benchmark overhead against typical practices
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Model the ROI on new equipment
tasks.detail: Calculate net revenue per case, monthly profit contribution, break-even cases per month, and the payback period in months, and finish with a one-line verdict.
followups: How many cases a month is break-even?
Compare buying vs. leasing vs. waiting a year.
Which of my utilization assumptions is most likely wrong?

tasks.text: Compare buying, leasing, and financing equipment
tasks.detail: Show monthly cost, total cost, cash impact, and flexibility side by side, with a recommendation — and note that I'll confirm the tax treatment with my CPA.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Decide whether to repair or replace equipment
tasks.detail: Weigh age, reliability, downtime, and remaining useful life; show the cost-over-time comparison and the tipping point that would change the answer.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Spot price creep in my supply spend
tasks.detail: Identify items whose unit cost rose, quantify the annual dollar impact of each, and rank them — with a suggested action for each one: renegotiate, substitute, or switch.
tasks.input: ordering data for this period and last period
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Design and price an in-house membership plan
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

roles.label: Expansion analyst (locations & startups)
roles.prompt: a dental-practice expansion and site-selection analyst
contexts: the current office is at capacity
reviewing a lease or letter of intent
a typical month at the practice

tasks.text: Evaluate a potential second location
tasks.detail: Look at demographics, daytime and traffic patterns, the dentist-to-patient ratio, and nearby competitors — including how established they are (e.g. estimate the owners' career stage from their dental-school graduation year).
followups: What data would change this from no-go to go?
Estimate first-year cash flow for this location.
What are the three biggest risks, and how would I de-risk each?

tasks.text: Screen available properties on Crexi or LoopNet
tasks.detail: Search sources like Crexi and LoopNet for available or for-lease properties that would suit a dental office, and summarize the best options with size, price, and location notes. Screen each for dental fit — plumbing capacity, electrical, ADA access, parking — and flag every listing 'confirm directly; verify dental suitability in person'.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Analyze lease terms and flag risky clauses
tasks.detail: Translate the terms into plain English, estimate the effective rent over the full term, and flag risky clauses — escalations, CAM charges, personal guarantees, exclusivity, demolition or relocation rights. My attorney reviews before anything is signed.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Build defensible pro-forma assumptions for a startup
tasks.detail: Cover chairs, days open, new patients per month, ramp curve, fee schedule, payer mix, staffing, and overhead ratios — with reasonable starting ranges and a note on which numbers to localize.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Estimate startup cost and break-even for a new location
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Decide between starting fresh and acquiring
tasks.detail: Give an honest pros-and-cons for each path in my situation, the break-even-time and risk differences, and a recommended path with the conditions that would change it.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

roles.label: Acquisition & deal advisor (buying & financing)
roles.prompt: a dental-practice acquisition and deal advisor
contexts: eyeing a specific practice to buy
building a list of acquisition targets
a typical month at the practice

tasks.text: Estimate what a practice is worth
tasks.detail: Use multiple methods — percent of collections, an EBITDA multiple, and an SDE multiple — show the range each gives and what drives it, then give a blended view and the factors that would push value up or down. Treat this as a starting estimate to confirm with a qualified valuator.

fields.label: Asking price (if there is one)
fields.sentence: The asking price is {v} — sanity-check it against your estimate

followups: Which factors here would move the value most?
What documents should I request to tighten this estimate?
Model the value if collections grow 10% under my ownership.

tasks.text: Model whether a deal's cash flow covers the debt
tasks.detail: Model projected cash flow, annual debt service, DSCR, and owner take-home in a base and a conservative case. End with a clear 'can I afford this' verdict and the single assumption the answer is most sensitive to.
tasks.input: deal numbers (price, financing terms, and the practice's cash flow)
followups: Rerun it with a 15% production dip in year one.
What loan terms would make this deal comfortable?
What's my realistic monthly take-home in year one?

tasks.text: Scan a listing or prospectus for red flags
tasks.detail: Look for declining production, heavy owner-dependency, concentrated payers, deferred maintenance, lease risk, and vague add-backs. Rank the red flags and give the question each one raises before any letter of intent.
followups: Draft the questions to send the broker about each red flag.
Which single red flag would you walk away over?
What would a clean version of this deal look like?

tasks.text: Scrutinize the seller's add-backs (quality of earnings)
tasks.detail: Normalize the earnings, flag any add-back that looks aggressive or unsupported, and list the documents to request to verify each one.
tasks.input: financials and the seller's add-back list
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Draft a respectful outreach letter to an owner-dentist
tasks.detail: No pressure, no lowball — a warm, confidential opening from one dentist to another about whether they'd ever consider a transition. Under 150 words, with [name], [your name], and [contact] placeholders.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Build a first-call question list for a selling dentist
tasks.detail: Give me 12 to 15 questions grouped by theme — motivation, financials, team, patients, real estate, timeline — with a note on what a good versus a concerning answer sounds like.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Draft a non-binding letter-of-intent framework
tasks.detail: Cover price and structure, assets included, contingencies, transition terms, exclusivity, and timeline with [placeholders] throughout — clearly labeled as a starting template for my attorney and CPA to finalize.
followups: List the negotiation levers beyond price.
What should my opening position be?
What do sellers most often push back on?

tasks.text: Build a due-diligence master checklist
tasks.detail: Cover financial, clinical, legal, operational, staff, real-estate, and IT areas — with the documents to request and the deal-breakers to watch for in each.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Estimate the all-in cash needed beyond the purchase price
tasks.detail: Include working capital, transition costs, deferred maintenance, marketing, and a reserve — and call out the items buyers most often underestimate.
tasks.input: practice details (size, condition, payer mix, current marketing)
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Compare two acquisition targets side by side
tasks.detail: Compare price, production, payer mix, location, growth potential, and risk, then give a reasoned 'which and why' — noting any data gaps that could change the call.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Plan the first 100 days after buying a practice
tasks.detail: Phase it days 1–7, 8–30, and 31–100 across systems, team, patients, billing, and branding — and highlight the items most likely to disrupt patients if missed.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

roles.label: Business coach / advisor
roles.prompt: a business coach for a dental-practice owner
contexts: feeling stretched thin as the owner
unsure what to focus on next
working in the business, not on it
a typical month at the practice

tasks.text: Vision organizer: help me figure out what I really want
tasks.detail: Interview me one question at a time to uncover what I actually want from the practice and my life — more time, money, freedom, or meaning — and what's getting in the way. Ask 6–8 thoughtful questions, waiting for my answer each time, then summarize what you heard and give me a short, prioritized action list to get there.
followups: Turn the action list into a 90-day calendar.
What should I stop doing entirely?
What's the first thing I should delegate, and to whom?

tasks.text: Define my weekly owner KPI dashboard
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Build an annual goals and budget framework
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Run a quarterly SWOT and tell me where to focus
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Assess what would raise my practice's value before a sale
tasks.detail: Score owner-independence, systems, growth, margins, and payer mix; give a prioritized action list ranked by impact on the multiple, focused on the 12-month moves that pay off most.
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Brainstorm ventures that fit my skills and assets
tasks.detail: Give 10 dental-adjacent ideas with the angle, why I'm suited to each, and a rough effort-versus-return read. Be candid about which are crowded.
tasks.input: list of your assets and skills (practices, capital, network, interests)
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
What would you need from me to make this more precise?
Turn your top recommendation into concrete next steps for this week.

tasks.text: Pressure-test a business idea
tasks.detail: Assess the market size, the real customer, the competition, the moat, and the top risks. Give an honest pursue / shape / pass verdict, the biggest assumption to test first, and a cheap way to test it before I invest.
tasks.input: idea (what it is, who it's for, how it makes money)
followups: What's the single highest-impact item here, and why?
